title: carex riparia var. lacustris
intro: Carex riparia var. lacustris, also known as lake sedge or lacustrine sedge, is a type of perennial aquatic plant that belongs to the sedge family. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Carex riparia var. lacustris include reducing inflammation, acting as an antiseptic, and supporting the immune system. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
